events.text: Every machine connection begins with a configuration profile -- it holds SSH keys, server addresses, and infrastructure settings. We create a new profile now, linked to your private key.
Profile created. We list all configuration profiles on the system to see the new one alongside any existing profiles.
Next we register a remote machine -- providing its IP address and SSH user. Host keys are scanned automatically for trusted access.
We list the machines configured in this profile to confirm the worker was added with the correct connection details.
Time to test connectivity. We run a remote command over SSH and confirm the machine's hostname is returned.
One more check -- we query the machine's uptime and load average to make sure it is responsive and healthy.
We run a full diagnostic scan covering the CLI environment, renet installation, configuration, and subscription status in one command.
With connectivity proven, we configure the machine's infrastructure -- public IP, base domain for routing, and certificate email for automated TLS.
We display the saved infrastructure configuration to confirm the IP, domain, and certificate settings were stored correctly.
